inventoryPolicy.stockCaution: This is a product reference for what is typically bought or used, not a live stock-count system.
inventoryPolicy.reorderLogic: Use seasonal timing, property area math, and purchase history to decide what to buy ahead of work windows.

notes: A 5,000 sq ft product covers about 61% of the full lawn.
Two 5,000 sq ft units are typically needed for full-property coverage.

categories.label: Pre-emergent
categories.storeQuestion: What do I buy before crabgrass season?
categories.defaultTiming: Buy February-March, apply around the April redbud/full-bloom window if needed.

categories.label: Fertilizer
categories.storeQuestion: What feeds the lawn without pushing the wrong season?
categories.defaultTiming: Prioritize fall feeding; use spring fertilizer carefully.

categories.label: Weed control
categories.storeQuestion: What do I use only when weeds justify it?
categories.defaultTiming: Spot or selective use only when label weather conditions are right.

categories.label: Seed / patch
categories.storeQuestion: What tall fescue repair product do I usually use?
categories.defaultTiming: Plan late July-August, use mainly in September or for small patch repairs.

categories.label: Soil / topdressing
categories.defaultTiming: Buy only for a defined repair, leveling, or seed-support plan.

categories.label: Mulch
categories.storeQuestion: What mulch do I usually buy?
categories.defaultTiming: Review beds in spring; large refresh is likely every other year.

categories.label: Pest control
categories.storeQuestion: What bug and wasp products do I usually use?
categories.defaultTiming: Use when pests justify it; avoid routine broad spraying without a reason.

categories.label: Consumables
categories.defaultTiming: Add blades, line, oil, filters, batteries, chain oil, or similar parts as they become known.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Halts Crabgrass Preventer with Lawn Food
items.typicalUse: Crabgrass prevention with lawn food when prevention is planned.
items.spreaderSetting: Unknown; use Scotts Elite label guidance and record actual setting.
items.useTiming: April around redbud/full-bloom timing if crabgrass prevention is planned
items.notes: One 5,000 sq ft unit covers about 61% of the full lawn; two units are typically needed for full-property coverage.
items.shortNote: Usual spring pre-emergent. Plan around 8,225 sq ft total lawn area.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder WinterGuard Fall Weed & Feed
items.typicalUse: Fall feeding with weed control when label conditions and weed pressure justify it.
items.spreaderSetting: Unknown; use Scotts Elite label guidance and record actual setting.
items.useTiming: Fall window, generally after summer stress and before winter closeout
items.reorderTiming: Buy ahead of September-November fall feeding windows
items.notes: Treat as a 5,000 sq ft working product for property math.
items.shortNote: Fall product. Two 5,000 sq ft units usually cover full-property planning.

items.name: Scotts EZ Seed Patch and Repair Tall Fescue
items.typicalUse: Tall fescue patch repair and small thin areas.
items.useTiming: Best for fall repair windows or small patch work when watering follow-through is realistic
items.reorderTiming: Late July-August if fall patching is planned
items.notes: Patch product, not a whole-lawn renovation plan.
items.shortNote: Use for targeted tall fescue repairs.

items.name: Ortho Weed B-gon Lawn Weed Killer + Crabgrass Control
items.typicalUse: Selective lawn weed and crabgrass control when label conditions fit.
items.useTiming: Use only when weeds justify treatment and weather conditions fit the label
items.reorderTiming: Do not reorder automatically; decide based on weed pressure
items.notes: Keep separate from non-selective weed killer workflow.

items.name: Roundup Weed & Grass Killer
items.typicalUse: Non-selective weed and grass killing where turf or desirable plants should not survive.
items.useTiming: Use carefully on calm days away from desirable turf and plants
items.reorderTiming: Buy only when non-selective control is needed
items.notes: Pump Sprayer #1 is reserved for this product.
items.shortNote: Non-selective. Keep away from lawn turf.

items.name: Roundup For Lawns
items.typicalUse: Grass-safe lawn weed control when weeds justify spot treatment.
items.useTiming: Use when label weather conditions fit and lawn weeds justify it
items.reorderTiming: Do not reorder automatically; decide by actual weed pressure
items.notes: Pump Sprayer #2 is reserved for grass-safe weed killer.

items.name: Cutter Backyard Bug Control Spray Concentrate
items.typicalUse: Outdoor bug control when pest pressure justifies treatment.
items.useTiming: Use when pests justify it and label conditions fit
items.notes: Avoid routine broad spraying without a reason.

items.name: Ortho Home Defense Insect Killer
items.typicalUse: Bug spray around appropriate outdoor/perimeter areas according to label.
items.useTiming: Use only where pest control is needed and label conditions fit
items.reorderTiming: Buy when bug-spray workflow needs refill
items.notes: Pump Sprayer #3 is reserved for this product.

items.name: Wasp and Hornet Foam Spray
items.typicalUse: Nest treatment where wasps or hornets create a practical hazard.
items.useTiming: Use only for confirmed wasp/hornet problems and follow label safety directions
items.reorderTiming: Keep in mind during active wasp/hornet season if prior can is used
items.notes: Situational safety product, not routine landscape treatment.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Lawn Soil
items.typicalUse: Small repairs, leveling, and seed support where useful.
items.useTiming: Small repair areas, leveling, or seed support where useful
items.reorderTiming: Buy only for a defined repair or seed plan
items.notes: Do not treat bagged soil as a substitute for solving compaction or irrigation issues.
items.shortNote: Repair material, not a whole-yard fix.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Tall Fescue Mix
items.typicalUse: Tall fescue spot repairs and small thin areas.
items.useTiming: Best saved for September thin spots or small repairs
items.reorderTiming: Late July-August if fall seeding is planned
items.notes: Fall seeding is easier than spring/summer establishment around Valley Center, KS.
items.shortNote: Receipt-history seed product; EZ Seed is the current typical patch reference.

items.name: Scotts Turf Builder Weed & Feed5
items.typicalUse: Conditional weed control plus feeding when weeds and label conditions justify it.
items.spreaderSetting: Unknown; follow label and record actual setting.
items.useTiming: Only when weeds and label conditions justify it
items.reorderTiming: Do not reorder automatically; decide based on actual weed pressure
items.notes: Not the main current fall product reference; WinterGuard 50240 is the current working fall product.
items.shortNote: Historical receipt item, not the default fertilizer plan.

items.name: Premium Dark Brown Mulch
items.reorderTiming: Review every other year or when beds visibly need refresh
items.notes: Large 2025 purchase supports an every-other-year planning assumption unless conditions change.
items.shortNote: Known mulch choice from the 2025 bulk refresh.
